name: Croatian
blurb: Croatian is a South Slavic language spoken by ~5 million people in Croatia, Bosnia and beyond. It's written exactly as it sounds, so once you learn the alphabet you can read anything, and it's mutually intelligible with Serbian, Bosnian and Montenegrin.
paretoSummary: 80% of useful Croatian comes from a focused core: the fully phonetic alphabet (learn it once and pronunciation is solved forever), the present tense of biti (to be) and a few verb patterns, and just two of the seven cases to start, nominative (the subject/dictionary form) and accusative (the direct object). Add the most frequent ~600 words, basic word order (flexible thanks to cases), and an early feel for verb 'aspect' (one verb for ongoing actions, a partner for completed ones). Master this 20% and you can read aloud, ask and answer, and handle daily life, then layer in the remaining cases gradually.
